title: Cephalaria oblongifolia

The genus Cephalaria is part of the order Dipsacales, a group of flowering plants that also includes the honeysuckle and valerian families. The exact classification of Cephalaria oblongifolia may also include the class Magnoliopsida, which is a group of flowering plants that includes the majority of modern flowering plants.

intro: Cephalaria oblongifolia, also known as Mountain Blazing Star, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Asia and Europe, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the relief of respiratory issues and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Cephalaria oblongifolia include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cephalaria oblongifolia include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma.
